SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk

Class TerritoryOwnerView

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.risk.TerritoryOwnerView
All Implemented Interfaces:
Keyed
Direct Known Subclasses:
TerritoryOwner
public class TerritoryOwnerView extends Object implements Keyed
Stores information about territory ownership... e.g. who owns a territory.

You can think of a Territory as an enum, or some constant. e.g. there are a constant number of territories, and they might be the same across multiple different Game instances. Then a TerritoryOwnerView (this class) is an instance of that territory, which describes who exactly owns 1 specific Territory in a game.

  • Constructor Details

    • TerritoryOwnerView

      public TerritoryOwnerView(Territory territory)
      Creates an unclaimed territory owner with 0 armies.
      Parameters:
      territory - The territory this owner represents.

    • TerritoryOwnerView

      public TerritoryOwnerView(Territory territory, int ownerId, int armies)
      Creates a territory owner with the given owner and army count.
      Parameters:
      territory - The territory this owner represents.
      ownerId - The owning agent's id, or IAgent.NO_AGENT_ID for unclaimed.
      armies - The number of armies present.

    • TerritoryOwnerView

      public TerritoryOwnerView(TerritoryOwnerView copy)
      Copy constructor.
      Parameters:
      copy - The state to copy from.

  • Method Details

    • getTerritory

      public Territory getTerritory()
      Which constant territory is owned.
      Returns:
      The non-null owned territory.

    • getOwner

      public int getOwner()
      Returns the owner of this territory, or -1 for nobody yet (usually during game setup).
      Returns:
      Who owns the territory.

    • getArmies

      public int getArmies()
      How many units are currently occupying this territory.
      Returns:
      The number units, or 0.

    • isUnclaimed

      public boolean isUnclaimed()
      Returns true iff this territory has no owner.

      During Board setup (at the start of the game), all territories will be unclaimed before agents get to choose their territories.

      During a game, a territory may be unclaimed until an agent conquers the armies on that territory. This usually only happens in 2 player games of risk.

      Returns:
      if this territory has not been claimed.

    • id

      public int id()
      The unique integer, indexed at 0, of this object.

      This is the equivalent of calling Enum.ordinal(). This can be safely used as a "perfect hash."

      Specified by:
      id in interface Keyed
      Returns:
      The unique idx of this object.

      The unique name, case-sensitive, of this object.

      Returns:
      The non-null name of this object.
